symbols.name: AbstractReelBuilderFactory
symbols.detail: [USED] Transitively used by StandardReelBuilderFactory | [UNIQUE] No RAG data available | [OK] Abstract class correctly defines the contract; no logic, only a type-level declaration. | [ACCEPTABLE] ADR-002 (.anatoly/docs/02-Architecture/04-Design-Decisions.md) explicitly documents this abstract base as a deliberate design choice: it enables test-double factories for deterministic grid generation and documents the construction contract. However, the pre-computed usage analysis shows 0 importers for the abstract class itself, meaning nothing currently type-checks against the abstraction — only the concrete subclass is consumed. The ADR itself acknowledges the downside ('adds one layer of indirection for what is currently a trivial loop'), keeping this at ACCEPTABLE rather than LEAN.

symbols.name: StandardReelBuilderFactory
symbols.detail: [USED] Runtime-imported by 1 file: src/engine.ts | [UNIQUE] No RAG data available | [OK] Loop correctly iterates 0…reelCount-1 calling spinReel(i); unused _rowCount is a documented design decision per ADR-002 (reel height implicitly fixed by spinReel). No logic or type errors present. | [LEAN] The concrete implementation is a straightforward loop over spinReel(i) — minimal, does one thing well. Its single consumer is consistent with the project being a focused slot engine. The unused _rowCount parameter is an acknowledged consequence of spinReel fixing reel height implicitly (documented in ADR-002), not gratuitous complexity.

file_level.general_notes: Best practices score: 9/10 (0 FAIL, 2 WARN, 15 PASS)

rules.detail: All parameters and return types are explicitly annotated (reelCount: number, _rowCount: number, Symbol[][]). No implicit-any patterns visible; strict mode appears compatible. Cannot inspect tsconfig.json from provided context but no violations are detectable in this file.
No explicit or implicit `any` usage. All types are concrete: Symbol[][], number.
No union types defined in this file. The factory abstraction uses class inheritance, which is appropriate for this pattern. No type assertions present.
No obvious missed opportunities for Pick, Omit, Partial, etc. The method signatures are minimal and purpose-built. Not applicable here.

The abstract method signature returns `Symbol[][]` (mutable array of mutable arrays). In a regulated casino/gambling domain (inferred from reels, jackpot, paytable, freespin vocabulary and the ADR mentioning certification), returning a mutable grid from the factory contract risks accidental downstream mutation that could affect game integrity. The return type should be `readonly (readonly Symbol[])[]` to enforce immutability at the contract level.
Consistent use of abstract classes for the factory contract. The imported Symbol type originates in types.ts. No mixed interface/type alias inconsistency within this file.
The unused `rowCount` parameter is correctly prefixed with an underscore (`_rowCount`), satisfying the no-unused-vars rule. No other lint issues visible.

Both exported symbols — `AbstractReelBuilderFactory` and `StandardReelBuilderFactory` — lack JSDoc comments. The abstract `buildReels` method, which is the public contract for all factory implementations, has no documentation explaining the parameter semantics (especially the currently-unused `rowCount`, which the ADR notes is implicitly fixed at 3 by spinReel). This is particularly important for a class intended to be extended by test doubles and alternative implementations.
No deprecated APIs or outdated patterns. Uses .js extensions in imports (ESM-compatible). Abstract class pattern is idiomatic TypeScript.

Two imports, correctly grouped: type-only import first (`import type`), then value import. Both are local relative imports from the same src/ directory as expected per project structure. No external dependencies, no circular imports detectable, no side-effect imports.
No asynchronous code in this file. `buildReels` is fully synchronous. No unhandled rejections possible.

No hardcoded secrets, no eval, no command injection. The file is a pure structural factory with no I/O. RNG certification concerns (gambling domain inferred from reel/jackpot/paytable vocabulary and ADR-002 reference to certification) belong at the spinReel definition in reels.ts/rng.ts, not here per the consumer/owner rule.
Simple O(reelCount) loop with array push. No N+1 queries, no unnecessary re-renders, no synchronous I/O in async context.

The abstract factory pattern is documented in ADR-002 (.anatoly/docs/02-Architecture/04-Design-Decisions.md) as the primary testability mechanism — consumers inject any factory implementation, allowing test doubles to return deterministic hand-crafted grids. This is low coupling by design. No concerns at this layer.
No obvious missed opportunities for `satisfies`, const type params, or `using`. The file is a minimal two-class module where these features add no meaningful value.

The abstract factory is well-suited to this casino slot machine domain (five-reel grid generation pre-payline evaluation, as documented in ADR-002). The unused `_rowCount` parameter is an acknowledged design consequence per the ADR. No React or API-specific concerns apply.

suggestions.description: Make the factory contract return immutable grids to prevent accidental mutation of the symbol grid downstream — especially important in a regulated gaming context where grid state integrity must be guaranteed.
suggestions.before:
abstract buildReels(reelCount: number, rowCount: number): Symbol[][];

buildReels(reelCount: number, _rowCount: number): Symbol[][] {
suggestions.after:
abstract buildReels(reelCount: number, rowCount: number): readonly (readonly Symbol[])[];

buildReels(reelCount: number, _rowCount: number): readonly (readonly Symbol[])[] {

suggestions.description: Add JSDoc to both public exports, documenting the contract and the known rowCount caveat (currently unused, implicitly fixed at 3 by spinReel).
